title: MRD Resistance Geometry Second-Source Task Queue v0

tasks.task_title: Add a second public source extraction for MRD trajectory split
public_deliverables: One new source-specific mechanism extraction record mapping at least one signal to mrd-trajectory-split-v0.
A source note preserving MRD method, specimen, threshold, time point, and paired-sample context when available.
An updated MRD Resistance Geometry Coverage Report after the extraction is accepted.
acceptance_checks: The extraction uses the mechanism extraction schema and passes python3 tools/validate_public_artifacts.py.
Any new source is added to the public source registry before its source_id is referenced.
MRD trajectory language remains source-specific and does not become patient-specific MRD interpretation.
Coverage counts are described only as public artifact coverage, not mechanism frequency or evidence strength.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer relapse risk from MRD trajectory labels.
Do not compare MRD assays or thresholds unless the source provides aligned measurement context.
Do not recommend treatment changes from MRD trajectory evidence.

tasks.task_title: Add a second public source extraction for residual transcriptional adaptation
public_deliverables: One new source-specific mechanism extraction record mapping at least one signal to residual-transcriptional-adaptation-v0.
A source note separating malignant plasma cell state, therapy exposure, assay method, and posttreatment timing.
An updated MRD Resistance Geometry Coverage Report after the extraction is accepted.
acceptance_checks: The extraction uses the mechanism extraction schema and passes python3 tools/validate_public_artifacts.py.
The selected source supports residual transcriptional adaptation or an equivalent source-defined residual state signal.
Review-level summaries are not treated as cohort-level measurements unless the source provides the underlying measurements.
The task does not upgrade transcriptional adaptation into treatment-selection evidence.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer clinical actionability from transcriptional-state labels.
Do not rank residual-state mechanisms without aligned source extraction.
Do not treat scRNA-seq findings as routine clinical decision support.

tasks.task_title: Add a second public source extraction for genetic and nongenetic coupling
public_deliverables: One new source-specific mechanism extraction record mapping at least one signal to genetic-nongenetic-coupling-v0.
A source note preserving clone inference method, transcriptional-state method, and paired-sample context when available.
An updated MRD Resistance Geometry Coverage Report after the extraction is accepted.
acceptance_checks: The extraction uses the mechanism extraction schema and passes python3 tools/validate_public_artifacts.py.
The selected source supports a link between clone architecture and nongenetic or transcriptional state.
Correlation language is preserved and not converted into causal or clinical actionability language.
Coverage counts are not used as mechanism rankings.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer causality from correlation.
Do not rank genetic versus nongenetic mechanisms from one extraction.
Do not use this task for patient-specific prognosis or treatment selection.

tasks.task_title: Add a second public source extraction for metabolic resistance state
public_deliverables: One new source-specific mechanism extraction record mapping at least one signal to metabolic-resistance-state-v0.
A source note preserving assay method, cell-state context, pathway signal, and posttreatment context.
An updated MRD Resistance Geometry Coverage Report after the extraction is accepted.
acceptance_checks: The extraction uses the mechanism extraction schema and passes python3 tools/validate_public_artifacts.py.
The selected source supports a metabolic resistance or residual-cell metabolic-state signal.
Metabolism is framed as research extraction context, not intervention evidence.
Coverage counts are not used as mechanism-frequency estimates.
what_not_to_infer: Do not recommend metabolic interventions.
Do not infer that a metabolic state is dominant across relapse settings.
Do not translate pathway signals into clinical actionability without expert review.

tasks.task_title: Add a second public source extraction for NF-kB selective state
public_deliverables: One new source-specific mechanism extraction record mapping at least one signal to nfkb-selective-state-v0.
A source note preserving pathway-state context, selective-cell context, and assay method.
An updated MRD Resistance Geometry Coverage Report after the extraction is accepted.
acceptance_checks: The extraction uses the mechanism extraction schema and passes python3 tools/validate_public_artifacts.py.
The selected source supports NF-kB pathway state in a relevant myeloma resistance or residual disease context.
Pathway-state language is not converted into drug-target guidance.
The task preserves uncertainty and expert-review need.
what_not_to_infer: Do not recommend targeting NF-kB.
Do not rank NF-kB against other pathway states from this queue.
Do not infer patient-specific biology from pathway-state extraction.

tasks.task_title: Add a second public source extraction for subclone diversity
public_deliverables: One new source-specific mechanism extraction record mapping at least one signal to subclone-diversity-v0.
A source note preserving clone/subclone measurement method, sample context, and progression or treatment context.
An updated MRD Resistance Geometry Coverage Report after the extraction is accepted.
acceptance_checks: The extraction uses the mechanism extraction schema and passes python3 tools/validate_public_artifacts.py.
The selected source supports subclone diversity or clonal heterogeneity in multiple myeloma.
Review-level context is not treated as a measured subclone denominator unless the source provides measurements.
The task does not create patient-specific prognosis claims.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer individual prognosis from subclone diversity.
Do not compare subclone diversity across sources without aligned methods.
Do not rank subclone diversity as a relapse mechanism from artifact coverage alone.

tasks.task_title: Add a second public source extraction for the single-cell translation gap
public_deliverables: One new source-specific mechanism extraction record mapping at least one signal to single-cell-translation-gap-v0.
A source note preserving implementation barriers such as cost, standardization, data complexity, clinical workflow, or ethics.
An updated MRD Resistance Geometry Coverage Report after the extraction is accepted.
acceptance_checks: The extraction uses the mechanism extraction schema and passes python3 tools/validate_public_artifacts.py.
The selected source supports clinical-translation or implementation-readiness limitations for single-cell technologies.
Implementation barriers remain separate from biological mechanism claims.
The task does not imply a test should be ordered for any individual.
what_not_to_infer: Do not claim single-cell profiling is routine clinical decision support.
Do not recommend any test or platform.
Do not treat implementation barriers as evidence against the underlying biology.

tasks.task_title: Add a second public source extraction for T-cell therapy pressure
public_deliverables: One new source-specific mechanism extraction record mapping at least one signal to t-cell-therapy-pressure-v0.
A source note preserving therapy class, target class, relapse setting, prior exposure, and sequencing uncertainty.
An updated MRD Resistance Geometry Coverage Report after the extraction is accepted.
acceptance_checks: The extraction uses the mechanism extraction schema and passes python3 tools/validate_public_artifacts.py.
The selected source supports T-cell-directed therapy pressure, target sequence, or immune-redirection context.
Product and target statements remain descriptive and are not converted into treatment comparisons.
Trial or regulatory context is not treated as efficacy, eligibility, availability, or safety guidance.
what_not_to_infer: Do not recommend CAR T, bispecific antibodies, targets, products, or treatment sequences.
Do not compare BCMA and GPRC5D approaches from this task.
Do not infer patient-specific trial fit or therapy fit.

tasks.task_title: Add a second public source extraction for unmet high-risk context
public_deliverables: One new source-specific mechanism extraction record mapping at least one signal to unmet-high-risk-context-v0.
A source note preserving disease context, risk context, subtype scope, and source limitations.
An updated MRD Resistance Geometry Coverage Report after the extraction is accepted.
acceptance_checks: The extraction uses the mechanism extraction schema and passes python3 tools/validate_public_artifacts.py.
The selected source supports high-risk, extramedullary, plasma cell leukemia, or related unmet-need context.
Context variables are not treated as causal mechanisms unless the source explicitly supports that interpretation.
The task does not create patient-specific risk, prognosis, eligibility, or treatment-fit guidance.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer patient-specific prognosis.
Do not recommend treatment or trial options for high-risk contexts.
Do not compare high-risk contexts without aligned source definitions.

review_notes: The MRD trajectory second-source task is now done through martinez-lopez-2020-mrd-dynamics-geometry-v0.
Eight ready tasks remain for second-source extraction across under-covered geometry nodes.
Task source_ids include pubmed as a discovery anchor where a second source has not yet been selected.
